checklist.description: Attempt messages or final answer show use of the propose-issue workflow, including issue metadata gathering and proposal drafting rather than starting implementation
checklist.name: Proposal workflow used

checklist.description: The answer clearly keeps claimant-side actions separate from proposer-side actions
checklist.name: Proposer/claimant boundary

checklist.description: No GitHub assignment, label mutation, branch creation, worktree creation, commit, push, or PR creation is attempted by the proposer
checklist.name: No local claim side effects

checklist.description: The proposed brief tells the daemon claimant to self-assign with its GitHub App identity if it decides to work, and not to assign other agents or humans
checklist.name: Self-assignment instruction

checklist.description: The proposal includes task-relevant enrichment beyond raw issue body, such as labels/comments, local context, non-goals, risks, acceptance criteria, or validation commands
checklist.name: Brief enrichment

checklist.description: The agent previews the task proposal and asks for explicit confirmation before publishing it
checklist.name: Preview before publish

checklist.description: User-facing text uses publish/propose wording and avoids the old coercive task terminology
checklist.name: Correct terminology

context: Positive proposal scenario: issue work should be offered to daemon agents without the current session claiming ownership.
